page_content: ENST334 Environmental Toxicology (3 Credits) Concepts and case histories in ecotoxicology. Emphasis on origin and variety of environmental pollutants, routes of biological exposure, modes of toxicological action and effects on individual organisms, populations and ecosystems. Ecotoxicological issues in the Chesapeake Bay will be used as examples. Prerequisite: CHEM131, CHEM132, and BSCI207; or permission of AGNR- Environmental Science & Technology department.
